## [00:00 - 03:02]

So let's talk about the seed launch and specifically the strategy behind the seed launch. Because this is, it seems like such a simple strategy, but it is so, so powerful. What I've noticed is that a lot of advanced people want to just jump right past it, or even people that are just starting out, they think, well that seed launch sounds cool, but it doesn't look quite as fancy as the full product launch formula. Well, let me tell you, it is part of the product launch formula. It's super powerful. I've done seed launches recently after almost 20 years in this business. I did a seed launch and it was amazing. I learned all kinds of new things. So the key thing with a seed launch is that it revolves around having a conversation with your list and especially a conversation with the people that buy from you and it enables you to create a great product. At the end of the day, the best way to have a great business is to have a great product. And I see so many people struggling to create products and sometimes creating products that aren't great or they might be great, but they're not great for the people that bought from you. And the seed launch allows you to get by all of that, all of that. It's super, super powerful. So let's dig a little bit into the strategy. So it's a great place to start. If you don't have a list, if you don't have a product, this is the easiest way to create a product. One of the beautiful things is you create the product after you get paid for it. So you sell your program before you create it. Now people think that's really crazy. Like, how can you, isn't that unethical to sell something before you create it? Well the reality is that's the way much of the world works. You pay for your college classes before you take them. You pay for a play before you see it. You pay for a movie before you see it. You pay for a newspaper or a magazine before it's created. So this is something that exists in the real world. And you're just taking advantage of this process to basically get paid before you actually create it. It does all kinds of good things. It pushes you to create it. It forces you to create it. It's fantastic. When you do your seed launch, you create it live with a small group. It's super, super high value for them because of that. Because of the interactive nature. You deliver your content during live calls. Usually I suggest five calls. I've seen people do three. I've seen people do seven. Just do five. It'll make it simple for you. Do it over five calls. You send a survey between each call to identify what to teach in the next call. So this comes down to taking your topic and you divide it into five sub topics. And then you do a survey before each call to find out of that sub topic what do people

need to learn. Then you record the calls and you have them transcribed, which is easy. It's very inexpensive. And then you package your training into a digital product or a program to sell. Then you build a great product because it's based on audience input. It's not you just sitting somewhere in a dark room and trying to figure out what should be in the product. You base it on the audience. The whole interactive process makes it easy to build great relationships so people tend to get good results and that gives you great case studies. It's all just one beautiful cohesive piece. It's great when you're just starting out. The seed launch is great when you're just starting out because you learn how to teach. Because you might know how to do whatever your product is about, but you might not be great at teaching it. This is me with Product Launch Formula. I had been doing launches for nearly a decade. I was really good at doing launches, but I didn't necessarily know how to teach them. I did a seed launch. I learned how to teach them. What that means is once we get to a certain level of expertise, it's almost impossible for us to remember what it's like to be a beginner. But the seed launch strategy, the reality is you're mostly going to be selling to beginners. Any market, any market, the majority of the people are beginners because, well, it's just simple math. Everyone starts as beginners and then some of them move on to become intermediates, few of them move on to become experts, and few of them go on to be world-class at whatever it is. From horse training to baby psychology to computer programming to singing to whatever the topic is, there's more beginners than experts.
